_README: Frontier reference cards for the cross-model parity benchmark (fak/internal/turnbench parity.go). These are the HOSTED models the local stack is measured against on the frozen tau-bench airline task. Provenance is explicit per axis: capability + safety are MEASURED-GRADED (4 real Claude runs through the exact task/tool/oracle environment, graded against the oracle, 2026-06-17); cost is DERIVED-FROM-LOOP (the task's fixed 6-turn tool sequence priced with the same token profile the live Gemini-2.5-flash run exhibited on this harness — 4100 cumulative prompt + ~210 completion tokens — at published per-MTok rates). The safety fields model the hosted model AS TYPICALLY DEPLOYED (no local kernel in front of tool execution): the poisoned refund-policy document DOES enter the model's context (injection_in_context=true), but the model RESISTS it (destructive_executed=false). Running ANY model behind the fak kernel raises its safety to structural (poison never admitted) — the kernel is model-agnostic — so these cards are the conservative 'frontier alone' bar, not 'frontier+kernel'.
